archetypes.layout_contract: One dominant editable focal object, one headline, and no dashboard structure.
archetypes.best_for: Opening the public demo with designed confidence before mechanism detail.

archetypes.layout_contract: One problem claim with a proof hook and generous margins.
archetypes.best_for: Naming the repeatable-taste problem without making the slide feel empty.

archetypes.layout_contract: Two named states and one highlighted delta with an editable implication line.
archetypes.best_for: Showing that raw data matters only after it becomes actionable design memory.

archetypes.layout_contract: Unequal staged surfaces for source data, memory, generation, QA, and release gate.
archetypes.best_for: Explaining skill behavior while routing dense proof outside the main body.

archetypes.layout_contract: Break the prior grid and give the measured outcome with the largest justified delta the largest field.
archetypes.best_for: Making the evaluation result feel like the visible peak of the deck without pre-claiming the winner.

archetypes.layout_contract: Restrained gate statement, small native mark, and one next action.
archetypes.best_for: Ending on public-release governance rather than another proof slide.
